mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% in July 2021 reflects post-pandemic labor recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5%, creating negative real rates that incentivize equity allocation over cash, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% and CPIAUCSL at 272.265 in July 2021
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative real rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1.6 million annualized units, signaling robust residential investment and forward-looking construction demand that lifts cyclical equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1643 thousand units in June 2021
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong housing demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1600 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, indicating strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.0951 in June 2021 reflecting post-pandemic production recovery
regime_hint: supply-side recovery with expanding industrial capacity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.015 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive bias (mean return not significantly > 0 at 95% confidence) when INDPRO accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while unemployment rises simultaneously, signaling demand destruction and consumer retrenchment that reduces equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly change < -1.0% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 621340 million dollars in June 2021
regime_hint: demand shock with synchronized labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite joint demand and labor weakness.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100 thousand persons month-over-month after a period of expansion, signaling labor market momentum loss that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100 after 3 consecutive months > 200
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146821 thousand persons in July 2021
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deceleration preceding recession
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating a positive growth-rate differential that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19358.176 billion chained 2017 dollars in Q2 2021
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy supporting above-trend growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where growth exceeds 2.5% with FEDFUNDS < 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1.5 million units, indicating synchronized demand strength across consumer and residential sectors that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 621340 million dollars and HOUST at 1643 thousand units in June 2021
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential segments
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite joint retail and housing strength.
